questions.prompt: Which SDK surfaces should the implementation treat as must-pass for Cloudflare Workers compatibility?
questions.description: The research found separate protobuf surfaces in service protos, common proto JSON utilities, cloud operations, optional user protobuf payload converters, and the Node-native Temporal Worker runtime.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Make the client-facing packages safe for Cloudflare Workers: `@temporalio/client`, shared `@temporalio/common` proto utilities used by the client path, and `@temporalio/cloud`; preserve the existing workflow bundle boundary. Assess optional protobuf payload converters, but keep the Node-native `@temporalio/worker` runtime out of scope.

questions.prompt: What migration strategy should the plan prefer?
questions.description: The main implementation decision is whether to replace protobufjs runtime reflection, adapt protobufjs, or introduce a static generated runtime behind existing package APIs.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Prefer a staged, static generated protobuf implementation behind the existing `@temporalio/proto` facade. Keep public imports and TypeScript-facing shapes stable where practical, isolate generated output and adapters, and avoid relying on Cloudflare's startup-only eval compatibility flag.

questions.prompt: What compatibility bar should the implementation plan hold?
questions.description: This determines whether the plan can alter generated message APIs or must preserve existing `@temporalio/proto` consumers.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Preserve public package imports, exported namespaces, request/response type aliases, and service client behavior as much as practical. Private generated internals, build scripts, and implementation details may change if the facade keeps downstream code stable.

questions.prompt: How should the plan optimize for merging upstream Temporal SDK changes?
questions.description: This fork should remain easy to rebase against the official SDK.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Keep upstream source edits narrow, avoid rewriting unrelated packages, keep generated files and generation scripts clearly separated, add facade/adapters instead of broad call-site churn, and document any fork-only divergence.

questions.prompt: What verification should be required before the implementation is considered done?
questions.description: Cloudflare compatibility needs more than normal unit tests because the failure is runtime-policy-specific.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Require normal package tests/builds plus a Cloudflare-style smoke test that imports and exercises the client-facing protobuf path in an environment where `eval` and `new Function` are disallowed. Also add a static or bundled-artifact scan for dynamic code generation patterns in Cloudflare-targeted output.
